jokes.text: Could it be under affirmation? You have thousands of those.
jokes.setup: Hotel clerk can't find his reservation under 'confirmation'
jokes.punchline: Michael suggests 'affirmation' as if it's a filing system while revealing his self-help obsession
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael logic mixing up similar words while accidentally revealing his personality quirks. Well-integrated character moment.

jokes.text: She bought us sight unseen, like a mail order bride.
jokes.setup: Explaining Jo Bennett's acquisition of the company
jokes.punchline: Comparing business acquisition to mail order brides
jokes.explanation: Sharp analogy that's both inappropriate and oddly apt. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: Are we ugly? Are we smart? Are we cool? Are we too cool? Do we speak english?
jokes.setup: Wondering what Jo will think of them
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd concerns ending with 'do we speak English' while speaking English
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with absurdist ending. Michael's neurotic overthinking on display.

jokes.text: My husband and I are divorced, but I kept the mrs. Just to piss off the new wife.
jokes.setup: Correcting Michael about her title
jokes.punchline: Reveals petty motivation behind keeping 'Mrs.'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character introduction that's both funny and revealing. Sharp, economical, and memorable.

jokes.text: They love a good crotch. / Yeah, they sure do. / You should take that as a compliment. / Oh, I do.
jokes.setup: Jo talking about her dogs
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo exchange with both parties leaning into it
jokes.explanation: Predictable innuendo but well-executed with both characters playing into it.

jokes.text: I am treating you the same as a man for whom I would also not stand. Unless it was the president. Or judge judy.
jokes.setup: Jo asking why he doesn't stand when she enters
jokes.punchline: Oscar's formal equality reasoning with unexpected Judge Judy exception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar moment - logical, pretentious, then undercut by pop culture reference. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Who's this tall drink of sun tea?
jokes.setup: Jo is being introduced to office workers
jokes.punchline: Describes Jim as 'tall drink of sun tea' - a folksy Southern expression
jokes.explanation: Good character-establishing line that shows Jo's Southern personality through colorful language

jokes.text: Two guys doing one job? We gotta do something about that.
jokes.setup: Learning about the co-manager situation
jokes.punchline: Jo's matter-of-fact business assessment
jokes.explanation: Efficient setup for the episode's main conflict. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Jeez, you gave me a paper cut on my throat!
jokes.setup: Andy giving out Valentine's cards
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated reaction to paper cut placement
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy moment. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: Yeah, I have this thing about men cutting or threatening to cut my throat. Don't try to cut my throat.
jokes.setup: Reacting to the paper cut
jokes.punchline: Escalating to death threats while maintaining casual tone
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jo's intensity through absurd overreaction.

jokes.text: Which, from your description, sounds like a colorful, lawless swamp.
jokes.setup: Defending the co-manager system
jokes.punchline: Michael's inadvertently accurate description of Florida
jokes.explanation: Good verbal sparring with Michael accidentally being insightful about Florida.

jokes.text: Each of you is doing half a job. / No. / And sometimes I can hardly handle that.
jokes.setup: Jo assessing their work division
jokes.punchline: Michael's inadvertent admission of incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - trying to defend himself but accidentally revealing his limitations.

jokes.text: You can't give me gravy and tell me it's jelly, 'cause gravy ain't sweet... Is it, jim?
jokes.setup: Rejecting their explanation
jokes.punchline: Bizarre Southern metaphor with genuine uncertainty about gravy sweetness
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully weird Jo moment. The uncertainty about gravy makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Where I'm from, there're two types of folk-- Those who ain't, And those who are knee-high on a grasshopper. Which type ain't you ain't?
jokes.setup: Trying to out-Southern Jo
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical folk wisdom that makes no grammatical or logical sense
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael absurdity - trying to be folksy but creating complete gibberish. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: Do you even know what that means? / Yes. [followed by immediate dismissal]
jokes.setup: Jim calling out Michael's nonsense
jokes.punchline: Michael's confident lie immediately undermined by Jo dismissing them
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Michael's nonsense. The immediate dismissal adds to the cringe.

jokes.text: I'm joleen bennett. Jo for short. I'm a breast cancer survivor. Close, personal friends with nancy pelosi, And truman capote and I slept with three of the same guys.
jokes.setup: Formal introduction
jokes.punchline: Escalating from serious to political to bizarrely sexual
jokes.explanation: Excellent character establishment through unexpected escalation. The Truman Capote detail is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: Beep beep! / Beep beep! / 20 people dead in a pileup. There's blood everywhere!
jokes.setup: Playing traffic jam game
jokes.punchline: Kevin's immediate escalation to graphic fatalities
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin moment - taking innocent game to dark extreme. Good character integration.

jokes.text: You named them? / Uh, charles schulz did.
jokes.setup: Erin thinks Andy named Snoopy and Woodstock
jokes.punchline: Andy's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's innocence and Andy's pedantic nature.

jokes.text: It's roger federer for men. I sprayed some in there. / Andy, whoa! Thank you very much. / It's got pheromones in it.
jokes.setup: Erin commenting on the card's smell
jokes.punchline: Andy's oversharing about cologne and pheromones
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy oversharing moment. The pheromone detail adds to the cringe.

jokes.text: Just choosin' seats. Not getting married. / Chop-chop, little onion.
jokes.setup: Jim and Erin standing together
jokes.punchline: Jo's bizarre 'little onion' endearment
jokes.explanation: 'Little onion' is wonderfully weird and specific to Jo's character.

jokes.text: No, we know. Texas.
jokes.setup: Jo asking rhetorically if they know where Tallahassee is
jokes.punchline: Kevin's confident wrong answer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin moment - confidently wrong with great economy. Very character-specific.

jokes.text: I could manage my way around that. / They're the easiest to use printers in the market. / I will try to manage my excitement.
jokes.setup: Learning about selling printers
jokes.punchline: Michael's repeated emphasis on 'manage' words
jokes.explanation: Obvious wordplay but fits Michael's transparent manipulation tactics.

jokes.text: Camel cigarettes did the same thing with joe camel By making him look like a penis. I can't even go near a cigarette now Without thinking of a penis. And vice versa.
jokes.setup: Explaining his psychological manipulation strategy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre confession about sexual association with cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully inappropriate Michael overshare. The 'vice versa' makes it even weirder.

jokes.text: But I guess in most romantic comedies, The guy you're supposed to be with Is the one that you never thought of in that way. You might have even thought he was annoying Or possibly homosexual.
jokes.setup: Realizing Andy might like her
jokes.punchline: Applying rom-com logic ending with casual homophobia
jokes.explanation: Shows Erin's naive worldview through rom-com analysis. The casual bigotry reveals character limitations.

jokes.text: You know, some people say they're not in it for the money. Well, with all due respect to this job, I think I'm definitely in it for the money. And, quite honestly, the women.
jokes.setup: Talking about motivations for sales job
jokes.punchline: Jim's unusually blunt honesty about shallow motivations
jokes.explanation: Unexpected honesty from usually more diplomatic Jim. Good character moment.

jokes.text: * la la la la, hello, oscar *
jokes.setup: Michael being happy about regaining manager position
jokes.punchline: Singing greeting to Oscar in musical theater style
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael exuberance moment. Shows his theatrical personality.

jokes.text: Oh, that is my favorite ocean--I love it!
jokes.setup: Oscar mentions reading The Atlantic magazine
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks he means the Atlantic Ocean
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - enthusiastic but completely missing the point. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Manuel who?
jokes.setup: Oscar mentions 'the manual'
jokes.punchline: Michael thinks it's a person named Manuel
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael confusion moment. Good economy and character fit.

jokes.text: I...Would sleep in my office, And I would sexually harass people. / Why would you do that? / I'm turning myself in right now.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he'd be a bad manager
jokes.punchline: Bizarre specific predictions and preemptive confession
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly dark humor from Jim. The escalation to confession is well-done.

jokes.text: Oh! Michael marked his heights. He's...Grown.
jokes.setup: Discovering height marks on the door frame
jokes.punchline: Erin's innocent observation about adult Michael tracking his height
jokes.explanation: Great visual gag revealing Michael's childish nature. Erin's innocent delivery perfect.

jokes.text: You're 20 minutes late. / Um... I was at another dumpster. / Just admit you lost track of time.
jokes.setup: Ryan showing up late to secret meeting
jokes.punchline: Ryan's ridiculous excuse and Dwight's exasperation
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Ryan's casual lying vs Dwight's intensity.

jokes.text: I wish I had a lair.
jokes.setup: Planning diabolical plot against Jim
jokes.punchline: Dwight's wistful villain aspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - completely earnest villain wannabe. Great economy and character.

jokes.text: Course I seesaw. Mose and I seesaw all the time. / No. Uh, the movie. / Oh. Yeah. Great film. Almost as fun as going on a seesaw.
jokes.setup: Ryan asking if Dwight saw the movie 'Saw'
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal interpretation and comparison to seesaws
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight moment combining literal thinking with specific farm details. Well-structured wordplay.

jokes.text: That is the most idiotic thing I have ever heard in my life! / Well, suggest something else then, don't just-- / He's supposed to cut his leg off? / Don't just criticize my idea! / Think! / You think of something then!
jokes.setup: Dwight rejecting Ryan's Saw movie plan
jokes.punchline: Escalating argument between supposed co-conspirators
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing their incompetence as villains.

jokes.text: Like frodo. / Why don't you just let me handle the tolkien references, Okay, dumb jock? / Well, I think he can be corrupted. Like gollum. / Smeagol was corrupted And became gollum.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to manipulate the IT guy
jokes.punchline: Escalating nerd gatekeeping over LOTR references
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing their competitive nerdiness even while plotting.

jokes.text: Do you want me to spin you in your chair and make you dizzy? / Why would I want to do that? / It's a thinking technique. All the top executives do it. It keeps the brain moving, And a...Spinning brain is a working brain.
jokes.setup: Erin trying to help Jim as new manager
jokes.punchline: Bizarre made-up business advice delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin moment - completely sincere nonsensical advice. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I went by there the other day. Saw some of the women walking in. Not...Really my cup of tea. Actually, kevin, you might like it.
jokes.setup: Michael describing Curves (thinking it's a strip club)
jokes.punchline: Insulting Kevin by suggesting he'd like overweight women
jokes.explanation: Predictable fat joke. Character-appropriate for Michael but lazy writing.

jokes.text: You know, we really don't announce out loud Our sales that much. / Why not? It's part of the sales experience. / It's not really 1992 anymore.
jokes.setup: Michael loudly announcing his sale
jokes.punchline: Jim's era-specific critique of Michael's outdated approach
jokes.explanation: Good observation about generational differences in sales culture.

jokes.text: Obsessed with me much? / Well, everyone got one. / What did yours say? / friends are worth sharing a doghouse with.
jokes.setup: Kelly thinking Andy's romantic card was special
jokes.punchline: Revealing Andy gave Kelly the most generic friendship card
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff showing Kelly's self-centeredness being deflated.

jokes.text: I feel like such a fool For thinking that andy was only going after one girl. 'cause andy bernard is a playboy, And why shouldn't he be? He's got it all.
jokes.setup: Erin thinking Andy is pursuing multiple women
jokes.punchline: Describing dorky Andy as a 'playboy' who 'has it all'
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Erin's innocent misreading of the situation and Andy's character.

jokes.text: Listen. I know you have to say that, But we got a little problem here. There is a employee named jim halpert, And he's doi some terrible things, okay? He's molesting people via the internet, And we need to stop him.
jokes.setup: Trying to get Jim's password from IT
jokes.punchline: Escalating to false accusations of internet crimes
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight escalation but the sexual accusations feel uncomfortable rather than funny.

jokes.text: No, the cops called us.
jokes.setup: IT guy suggesting calling the cops
jokes.punchline: Dwight claiming the cops called them first
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's lie. Economic and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: No, this is geological. / I sent an email out to everyone in this area That this might be a side effect To my new allergy medication I'm on.
jokes.setup: Michael insisting the bad smell is geological
jokes.punchline: Phyllis revealing she warned everyone about her medication side effects
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Michael's denial being undermined by Phyllis's matter-of-fact explanation.

jokes.text: It's not 'cause of the smell. I'm just expecting a nosebleed.
jokes.setup: Everyone covering their noses due to smell
jokes.punchline: Creed's unrelated and ominous explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment - mysterious, dark, and economical. Classic character beat.

jokes.text: I didn't see you. / And you were there all along. / Well, I warmed it up for you. So should be good to go.
jokes.setup: Awkward bathroom encounter
jokes.punchline: Andy's inappropriate comment about warming up the toilet
jokes.explanation: Predictable bathroom humor but fits Andy's awkward character.

jokes.text: Believe me, if I got that card, We'd be in the bathroom doing it right...Now.
jokes.setup: Discussing Andy's Valentine card
jokes.punchline: Meredith's extremely inappropriate sexual comment
jokes.explanation: Very character-appropriate for Meredith but predictable shock humor.

jokes.text: Well, it's what you wanted. I really need my job back. We made a terrible mistake here.
jokes.setup: Jim and Michael realizing their position swap was a mistake
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret after getting what they thought they wanted
jokes.explanation: Good 'grass is greener' moment showing immediate buyer's remorse

jokes.text: We make a poster that says 'happy opposite day,' And she sees it on the way out-- / That's stupid.
jokes.setup: Trying to solve their management problem
jokes.punchline: Michael's childish 'opposite day' solution
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael solution - completely childish and impractical. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Hey, what was that movie Where their boss was within earshot, And they could have just gone and talked to her? / Lethal weapon? / That's it.
jokes.setup: Looking for movie reference for their situation
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they use the 'Lethal Weapon' approach to just talk to their boss
jokes.explanation: Clever meta joke about their tendency to overcomplicate simple solutions. Well-crafted.

jokes.text: And weirdly, I'm a good dog person, right, guys? See? They love me.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to prove he's better at something than Jim
jokes.punchline: Using dogs' affection as evidence of his people skills
jokes.explanation: Good Michael logic - using irrelevant evidence to support his argument.

jokes.text: And don't ride 'em. Lotta people try to ride 'em.
jokes.setup: Asking them to walk her dogs
jokes.punchline: Specific warning about people trying to ride the dogs
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully specific and absurd detail that feels very Jo. Great economy.

jokes.text: please understand this does not mean That I like you in any way. / You don't even like us as friends? / Phyllis, you guys are like my closest friends. I just mean I don't like like you. / What are we, five?
jokes.setup: Andy's mass email clarification
jokes.punchline: Creating more confusion and Phyllis calling out the childishness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Andy's awkwardness with Phyllis providing the voice of reason.

jokes.text: Boss for...What was it? Oh...4 1/2 hours? New record... Low. Previous record, henry rostin. Boss for nine years, four months.
jokes.setup: Dwight commenting on Jim's brief management tenure
jokes.punchline: Revealing the absurdly long previous record holder
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment with specific records and the extreme contrast in tenure lengths.

jokes.text: I can't help but think That something we did made this possible.
jokes.setup: Watching Michael contentedly back in his office
jokes.punchline: Dwight taking credit for their scheme's failure
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Dwight finding pride in their complete failure.
